summary: Matched the Leadead item source file, replacing many placeholder stubs in src/melee/it/items/itleadead.c with C implementations and adding the item-specific structs/prototypes needed to express them. The PR also corrected related typing/signature issues, including adding the leadead member to Item_ItemVars, defining itLeadead_ItemVars and itLeadeadAttributes, tightening an itlinkarrow prototype, and reducing mpLib_80056C54 to the arguments actually used. The author explicitly framed this as a tooling-assisted decomp experiment: Claude Code, custom Claude Skills, m2c enhancements for union fields/void pointer field types/struct copies, complexity sorting, and an automated checkdiff.py iteration loop produced most of the matches. decomp.dev reported 51 new matches and +8516 matched bytes.

key_files.role: Main matched source file; added Leadead item behavior/state/motion/collision functions and required includes.
Updated Leadead function prototypes, including typed Item_GObj signatures for newly matched functions.
Added itLeadead_ItemVars with observed offsets for per-item runtime state.
Added itLeadeadAttributes and nested itLeadeadAttr_x0 for article special attributes.
Added leadead to the Item_ItemVars union so code can use ip->xDD4_itemVar.leadead.
Adjusted mpLib_80056C54 definition to remove two unused float parameters.
Adjusted mpLib_80056C54 declaration to match the corrected shorter signature.
Updated Donkey Kong caller to the new mpLib_80056C54 signature.
Tightened one Link Arrow physical callback prototype from HSD_GObj* to Item_GObj*.

what_changed: Replaced a large set of placeholder comments in itleadead.c with matched C implementations for Leadead item initialization, animation, physics, collision, and state-transition helpers.
Added concrete Leadead item-var and attribute structs: itLeadead_ItemVars in itCharItems.h and itLeadeadAttributes/itLeadeadAttr_x0 in itCommonItems.h.
Added itLeadead_ItemVars leadead as a member of the item vars union in it/types.h, enabling fielded access such as ip->xDD4_itemVar.leadead.x48 instead of raw/unknown storage.
Introduced many imports in itleadead.c for camera, fighter callbacks, item helpers, item collision, map collision, random, JObj, math, and trig functions required by the matched code.

Matched functions cited by decomp.dev included it_802E8CD8, it_802EA478, it_802EAAEC, it_802EA804, it_802EAC8C, itLeadead_UnkMotion4_Coll, itLeadead_UnkMotion3_Anim, itLeadead_UnkMotion9_Anim, it_802EA6F4, it_802E8BCC, and many others.
Simplified mpLib_80056C54 from ten parameters to eight by removing two trailing float parameters, then updated the Donkey Kong caller and the header declaration accordingly.
Added or refined prototypes in itleadead.h for newly matched functions, including bool/void return types and Item_GObj* parameter types where evidenced in the diff.
decomp.dev reported 48.54% matched code overall for GALE01, +0.22%, +8516 bytes, with 51 new itleadead matches and small improvements to itleadead .sdata/.sdata2 and mpLib_80056C54.
smart_moves: The author first created matching scaffolding, then attacked one function at a time rather than trying to hand-decompile the entire file in one pass.
Using m2c enhancements to specify a union member let decompiled guesses use Item_ItemVars:leadead directly, which made item state accesses more structured and reduced ambiguity.
Using m2c void-pointer typing for Article.x4_specialAttributes as itLeadeadAttributes gave the generated code a usable attribute layout instead of repeatedly casting opaque pointers by hand.
Adding m2c support for single-assignment Vec3 struct copies targeted a common Melee idiom and avoided less natural per-field copies that can hurt matching/readability.
Sorting functions by complexity with easy_funcs.py created a low-risk order for automated matching and let simpler functions establish idioms/struct fields for harder ones.
The checkdiff.py loop automated missing import fixes, rebuilds, and objdiff-cli Unix diff comparison, making each candidate implementation cheaply testable.

The author documented that the only non-trivial manual decompilation was the negative dot product in it_802EA804, a useful boundary marker for what the automated process could and could not infer in this slice.

Static inline helpers were used to share repeated state-transition bodies while preserving match-sensitive code shape, as shown by helpers such as it_802E8F24_inline and itLeadead_UnkMotion2_Anim_inline.
decomp_lessons: For item files, define the item-specific union member and article-special-attributes struct early; this can convert many raw offset accesses into consistent fielded code and improve m2c output quality.

When a function consumes Article.x4_specialAttributes, provide a typed struct for that field to the decompiler rather than leaving it as void*; the PR explicitly used --void-field-type Article.x4_specialAttributes: itLeadeadAttributes.
For per-item runtime storage under xDD4_itemVar, add the union member in it/types.h and use the named member consistently, e.g. leadead.x48, leadead.x44, leadead.x50, leadead.xE50.
Automated decomp can work well when constrained by repeated compile/objdiff validation and a retry limit; this PR used checkdiff.py and had the agent ask for help after five unsuccessful attempts.
Small prototype/signature corrections outside the target file may be necessary to unblock matching; here mpLib_80056C54 and itLinkarrow_UnkMotion1_Phys typings changed as supporting cleanup.
Negative dot-product or vector math expressions can be the hard part even when surrounding control flow is easy; it_802EA804 is explicitly called out by the author as the manual non-trivial fix.
Use evidence from matched call sites to prune unused parameters: mpLib_80056C54 lost two trailing float parameters and callers were updated accordingly.
Preserving item callback signatures as Item_GObj* rather than generic HSD_GObj* improves type information for item code and can remove casts/ambiguity.
naming_conventions: Item-specific runtime structs follow the project pattern it<Name>_ItemVars, here itLeadead_ItemVars.
Article attribute structs follow an item-name pattern such as itLeadeadAttributes, with a nested placeholder-style substruct itLeadeadAttr_x0 when field purpose is still unknown.
Unknown fields retain offset-like names such as xDEC, xE14, xE30_state, xE34_a_offset, xE40_b_offset, xE4C_scale, and xE50 rather than invented semantic names.
Leadead item callbacks continue the existing pattern itLeadead_UnkMotion<N>_{Anim,Phys,Coll} for unknown motion states.
Address-based helpers retain it_802E.... names until stronger semantic names are known, e.g. it_802E8BCC, it_802EA804, it_802EAC8C.
Function prototypes in headers use address comments such as /* 2EA804 */ next to symbols, consistent with nearby item headers.
The PR prefers typed Item_GObj* for item functions where known, while some existing unrelated prototypes still use HSD_GObj*.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used m2c with --union-field Item_ItemVars:leadead to force the correct item union member in generated C.
Used m2c with --void-field-type Article.x4_specialAttributes: itLeadeadAttributes to type the article attributes pointer.
Used m2c support for assigning structs such as Vec3 in one assignment rather than field-by-field when that matched generated code better.
Used easy_funcs.py to sort functions by complexity before decompiling them.
Used a custom tools/checkdiff.py script that fixed missing imports, rebuilt the file, and ran objdiff-cli in Unix diff mode for iterative validation.
Used PAD_STACK annotations in several matched functions where stack shape required explicit padding.
Used small static inline wrappers for repeated transition bodies while maintaining match-sensitive generated structure.
For it_802EA804, the author specifically called out a manually solved negative dot product; the diff shows a helper returning -(a->x * b->x) - (a->y * b->y) - (a->z * b->z).
review_feedback: No inline review comments were present in the supplied context.
Reviewer r-burns approved the PR and praised the result, saying it was impressive enough that they would not have guessed Claude generated it without being told.
The approval comment specifically highlighted interest in learning the new m2c arguments and Claude Skills, calling the workflow powerful.
decomp.dev bot feedback confirmed the technical impact: 51 new matches and +8516 matched bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs by lukechampine that reuse the Claude Skills/m2c/checkdiff workflow described in PR #2076.
Search for subsequent renames of itLeadead_ItemVars fields such as x48, x44, x50, xE30_state, xE34_a_offset, and xE50 once semantics became known.
Check whether the m2c enhancements for --union-field, --void-field-type, and Vec3 struct assignment were later merged upstream.
Look for later changes to mpLib_80056C54 to confirm the shortened eight-argument signature remained stable.
Compare other item-file matching PRs that define Item_ItemVars union members early to see if this became standard practice.
